raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The broad request to make the draft stronger centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the work.","R1.2":"Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the motivation, method, results, and conclusion follow coherently from one another.","R1.3":"The draft's claims about recoverability and generalization need to be aligned with the reported pilot, calibration, and accuracy results.","R1.5":"Improving strength may involve reorganizing the motivation, methodological concern, findings, and implications at the paragraph or section level.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and argumentative force.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is always active and is especially relevant because several claims depend on limited reported results and temporal assumptions.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is always active and is central where the artifact makes broad claims about frontier systems and generalization.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is always active to ensure the projection does not rewrite or evaluate beyond the requested responsibility mapping.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because overstated evaluation claims could mislead research or deployment decisions.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is always active to preserve distinctions between the delegation, artifact text, reported evidence, and projected responsibilities."}}

rationale.R1.1: The broad request to make the draft stronger centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the work.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the motivation, method, results, and conclusion follow coherently from one another.
rationale.R1.3: The draft's claims about recoverability and generalization need to be aligned with the reported pilot, calibration, and accuracy results.
rationale.R1.5: Improving strength may involve reorganizing the motivation, methodological concern, findings, and implications at the paragraph or section level.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and argumentative force.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active and is especially relevant because several claims depend on limited reported results and temporal assumptions.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active and is central where the artifact makes broad claims about frontier systems and generalization.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active to ensure the projection does not rewrite or evaluate beyond the requested responsibility mapping.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because overstated evaluation claims could mislead research or deployment decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active to preserve distinctions between the delegation, artifact text, reported evidence, and projected responsibilities.
